Wiom Customer App · OS Flow Prototypes (HTML)

Customer flows — redesigned against the Customer OS

Three customer journeys — Renewal, Router recovery, and the Home-status spine — rebuilt against the Wiom Customer OS (all 8 OSes). Screens tagged Current are actual Figma exports (CA June Sprint 2026 + CA Final Dev), shown untouched; new OS surfaces are drawn in the app's own व्योम-chat style with Material Symbols Rounded icons. The app paradigm is kept — status & nudges in chat, transactional steps as screens. Each flow opens with an OS-coverage strip, and every screen is tagged with the source OS that authors it. Pick a flow below, scroll sideways →

New Net-new, fixes an OS gap Improved Existing, reworked Current Real Figma export, untouched chat delivered as a chat message (app paradigm) CON Continuity OS rule / window posture Continuity posture SQ SQ Driver quality-state Recovery Recovery OS case / compensation RS Relationship-State classification ARR At-Risk Resolution tool (T1–T8) CM Customer-Mgmt · identity / obligation Exit Exit OS state CIC vulnerability tone (not value)
verified

How to read this: Phases A–C are the real Figma renewal flow (CA June Sprint 2026), exported as-is — the full journey, including the payment outcome states (processing / success / pending) and the "pay via someone else" branch for customers without their own UPI/card. Continuity OS then adds (Phase D) the safety-nets its doctrine requires — it reframes expiry as a 15-day reservation window (not "service stopped"), makes nudges posture-aware, and runs a verification window (T+10–15) that clarifies intent without urgency. Recharge is cross-cut by 8 OSes — the posture on every nudge is authored by RS OS (Fragile→Softened, AT_RISK, Paused), the broken-net suppression by SQ Driver + Recovery OS, the resolution-not-sale handoff by ARR OS, and the tone by CIC (vulnerability, not value). Scroll sideways →

OSes running on this flow
Continuity — primary: reservation window, postures, A6 PayG (all) CM — connection picker, obligation settle, returning-customer (3) SQ Driver — quality truth: Unusable / Wiom-side (15) Recovery — open case → Recovery-first, grace-extension (15) RS OS — postures: Fragile→Softened, AT_RISK, Paused (14·17·18) ARR — AT_RISK → resolution, not a sale (T1·T5) (17) CIC — tone by vulnerability: first-cycle / early-life Exit — T+15 RESERVATION_EXPIRED handoff (16)
A · Reminder & recharge (live Figma)
1चैट: रिचार्ज से पहले रिमाइंडरCurrentchat
Real Figma — proactive pre-expiry nudge while net is still चालू: "अगला रिचार्ज करें · आपका नेट 25 दिसंबर, 4:15 pm पर ख़त्म हो जाएगा". Untouched.
chevron_right
2चैट: रिचार्ज नज (नेट रुका)Currentchat
Real Figma — व्योम chat with status banner + reminder + 1-day ₹25 recharge pack card. Untouched.
chevron_right
3अपने हिसाब से रिचार्ज चुनेंCurrentCM adds
Real Figma — duration picker (1/2/7/14/28/84/112/360 दिन). Untouched; CM OS adds a connection picker when the person holds more than one connection →
policyWhat CM OS adds here

If the person holds more than one connection, recharge must ask "कौन सा कनेक्शन?" and show the active SSID — so it lands on the right one. This is the documented fix for "रिचार्ज रिफ्लेक्ट नहीं हुआ" calls.

CM OS · multi-connection · C4

chevron_right
4कौन सा कनेक्शन? (CM)NewCM · multi-connection
New: if the person holds more than one connection, recharge must ask which one and show the active SSID — so payment lands on the right net. The documented fix for "रिचार्ज रिफ्लेक्ट नहीं हुआ" calls. Shown only when >1 connection exists.
New

policyCM OS · multi-connection · C4

CM OS is the only person-level OS — it knows every connection a customer holds. Recharge must resolve which service_instance before taking money, showing the SSID + live status. Default-select the active one, but never assume. This closes the "I recharged but my net didn't start" gap (wrong-connection payments).

CM OS · person-level · multi-connection · C4

B · Payment outcome (live Figma)
7भुगतान जाँचा जा रहा हैCurrent
Real Figma — payment polling: "धैर्य रखें · भुगतान की स्थिति जाँची जा रही है", footer "भुगतान पूरा होने तक इस स्क्रीन को बंद न करें". Untouched.
chevron_right
8भुगतान सफल हुआ!Current
Real Figma — full-screen success with green check: "भुगतान सफल हुआ! · अमाउंट: ₹150". Untouched.
chevron_right
9चैट: रिचार्ज सफलCurrentchat
Real Figma — returns to chat: "आपका रिचार्ज सफल रहा" + new validity. Untouched.
chevron_right
10भुगतान अभी तक बाकी है (edge)Current
Real Figma — payment-pending/failed dialog: "भुगतान अभी तक बाकी है! · ठीक है". The customer is returned to retry, not dropped.
policyContinuity OS · C4 · retry never costs the reservation

A failed or pending payment must never drop the customer or burn the reservation window. The recharge surface stays structurally accessible (C4) — retry, switch method, or ask someone else to pay. The relationship is unaffected by a payment hiccup.

Continuity OS · C4 · recharge always accessible

chevron_right
C · पैसे नहीं? किसी और से भरवाएँ — assisted payment (live Figma)
11UPI/Card नहीं? QR दिखाएँCurrent
Real Figma — "अगर आपके पास UPI/Card नहीं है": pay through a friend, family member, or nearby shopkeeper. Step 1 उन्हें QR दिखाएँ → Step 2 ₹xx0 का भुगतान करवा दें. Or "WhatsApp पर शेयर करें" if they're remote. Untouched.
policyContinuity OS · C4 · recharge for everyone

A huge share of Wiom's customers cannot pay digitally themselves — the recharge surface must still reach them. Pay-by-proxy (QR to a shopkeeper / link to a relative) is C4 in its truest form: the connection is never lost just because the account-holder lacks a UPI app. This is keep-the-connection-alive, operationalised.

Continuity OS · C4 · recharge surface always accessible

chevron_right
12WhatsApp: किसी से भरवाएँCurrentchat
Real Figma — pre-filled WhatsApp message with a payment link: "मैं Wiom का रिचार्ज कर रहा हूँ… अगर आप मेरे लिए भुगतान कर सकते हैं… मैं बाद में कैश/UPI से वापस कर दूँगा 🙏". Untouched.
chevron_right
13दोस्त के भुगतान का इंतज़ारCurrent
Real Figma — "अपने दोस्त के भुगतान पूरा करने का इंतजार करें" + "भुगतान पूरा होने पर यह बटन दबाएँ". Returns to success once the helper pays. Untouched.

D · Posture safety-nets — Continuity × RS × SQ/Recovery × ARR
14रिचार्ज खत्म → कनेक्शन सुरक्षितImprovedReservation T+0–15chatNormal
Fix: validity end framed as a neutral, reversible pause — not "नेट रुका हुआ है / service stopped". Net off, connection held, recharge restarts internet instantly.
Reframed

send
policyContinuity OS · C2 · reservation window

For 15 days after expiry, internet is off but the relationship stays alive — no "service suspended", no degraded "slow mode", no pressure. Recharge at any point restarts internet instantly and resets the window. Archetype: "Aapka recharge khatam ho gaya hai. Recharge karein toh turant chalu ho jayega."

Continuity OS · Scenario A (C2) · Reservation T+0–T+15

policyRS OS · Fragile → Continuity Softened

RS OS correlates accumulated damage across SQ, Recovery, and recharge cadence and emits the Fragile overlay. Continuity reads it (I7) and switches to the Softened posture — fewer, warmer nudges. RS only classifies; it never acts (Doctrine #12: Fragile ≠ AT_RISK). CIC layers vulnerability tone — RECENTLY_RESTORED means "we know this is the second time".

RS OS · Fragile overlay → Continuity Softened · CIC (vulnerability)

chevron_right

New: when SQ says the net is broken on Wiom's side, recharge nudges are suppressed — the customer is never asked to pay for internet that doesn't work.

policyContinuity OS · Recovery-first · C2

An open Recovery case (Wiom-side Unusable from SQ Driver) forces Recovery-first — all recharge prompts suppressed until the failure is resolved. If Recovery issues compensation, Grace-extension extends the window. The customer never pays for broken net.

Continuity OS · Recovery-first posture · C2

New: the verification window (T+10–15) clarifies intent — continue / pause / not-now — without any urgency. It exists for state clarity before the T+15 asset boundary, not to push a sale.

policyContinuity OS · Clarify-state-first

T+10–T+15 is a clarity window, not a sales window. Three neutral options. No "last chance", no countdown pressure. If no reaffirmation by T+15, RESERVATION_EXPIRED fires and Exit OS takes over — until then, the relationship is alive.

Continuity OS · Clarify-state-first · Verification Window

New: if a Fragile customer adds an exit-direction signal ("band karwa do", an escalated unresolved complaint), recharge nudges stop entirely. RS classifies AT_RISK and Continuity hands off to ARR — which resolves the problem, never sells.

यहाँ पूछें....
send
policyRS OS · AT_RISK → ARR (resolution, not retention)

Fragile + an exit-direction signal → RS emits AT_RISK; Continuity's mandatory handoff to ARR fires (I9). ARR is resolution, not retention — T1 names the specific deterioration (never "you're at risk"), T5 compensation is paid whether the customer stays or leaves (C3). No discounts, no "stay and get X", no save-coupons. A clean exit with the problem resolved is still a success.

RS OS · AT_RISK → ARR · I9 · T1/T5 · resolution-not-retention

send
policyRS OS · CUSTOMER_PAUSED → Continuity Passive-only · C4

RS OS authors CUSTOMER_PAUSED (explicit documented declaration only — never inferred from inactivity, Doctrine #24). Continuity reads it and goes Passive-only: completely silent. The recharge surface stays structurally accessible even here — Passive-only means we don't push, not that the customer can't pull. Payer-initiated payment is the only thing that clears it back to ACTIVE_ENTITLED.

RS OS · CUSTOMER_PAUSED → Continuity Passive-only · C4

verified

Router recovery / refund, anchored to the live Figma (CA June Sprint 2026, "Router pickup flow"). The Figma covers 5 time-window scenarios by how late the customer acts — and they map almost 1:1 onto the Exit OS refund matrix + reversibility zones. Screens tagged Current are real Figma exports. The flow is already strongly Exit-OS-aligned: it leads with the security deposit, offers self-return first (full ₹300), shows a fully transparent refund breakdown, and keeps recharge as a one-tap reversal in every window. Exit OS then adds the doctrine's safety-nets (stronger SD-first lead, explicit reversibility, custody-with-proof, closure bookend). Scroll sideways →

Reversibility timeline — refund shrinks as action gets later
CALM · R15–R18
Self-return ₹300 full. Recharge = instant reversal.
BOUNDED · R18–R46
Late self-return ₹200 → assisted pickup ₹200 (−50 pickup −50 late). Recharge → RECALL_CANCELLED, Wiom absorbs cost.
IRREVERSIBLE · >R46 / picked up
After pickup: physical reversal closed. Refund still owed — if no UPI, system keeps reaching out.
OSes running on this flow
Exit — primary: relationship + custody state machines, refund matrix (all) Continuity — recharge cancels the recall (reversibility) (11) CM — obligation ledger: ₹300 SD survives exit, refund-pending, welcome-back (9·13) RS OS — reflects EXITED for classification completeness; attribution at exit ARR — if exit followed at-risk-resolution-failed, carries the F1–F4 payload SQ / Recovery — feed exit attribution when the exit is deterioration-linked
A · Happy path — acted R15–R18 (Calm · ₹300 full)
1चैट: नेट-बॉक्स रिकॉल (SD-first)CurrentchatEXIT_INITIATED
Real Figma — recall already leads with the deposit: "नेट-बॉक्स वापस करें और अपने ₹300 वापस पाएं" + offers "1 दिन का रिचार्ज (₹25)" as a reversal. Exit-OS-aligned out of the box.
chevron_right
2वापस करने का तरीका चुनेंCurrentRECOVERY_IN_PROGRESS
Real Figma — "खुद से जमा करें · ₹300 पूरी सिक्योरिटी वापस" is offered first; "घर से पिकअप करवाएं" is secondary. Exactly the Exit OS refund matrix.
policyExit OS · refund matrix

Self-return earns the maximum tier (₹300 full) and is offered first — it aligns everyone (Wiom saves cost, customer gets full refund, fastest recovery). Refund tier is set by method + timing, never by customer "value".

Exit OS · Refund Matrix · self-return ₹300 in-time

chevron_right
3अपना UPI ऐप चुनें (रिफंड)Current
Real Figma — choose UPI app (PhonePe / Paytm / GPay) for the deposit refund. Untouched.
chevron_right
4खुद जमा करें — केयर सेंटरCurrent
Real Figma — व्योम Care Center address + directions/call, "नेट-बॉक्स वापस होते ही ₹300 वापस", "UPI ID देकर कन्फर्म करें". Untouched.
chevron_right
5चैट: UPI वेरिफाई + कहाँ जमा करेंCurrentchat
Real Figma — after UPI verify: "नेट-बॉक्स और केबल जमा करने पर ₹300 वापस · UPI वेरिफाई हो गई · कहाँ जमा करना है?". Untouched.
chevron_right
B · Later windows — refund shrinks (real Figma)
6देर से खुद जमा (R18–R25)CurrentchatLate · ₹300→₹200
Real Figma — missed the first window. The chat keeps the earlier ₹300 offer greyed (history) and adds: "आप देरी कर चुके हैं। फिर भी ₹200 वापस मिल सकते हैं, अगर 31 दिसंबर तक वापस कर दें।" Dignified, still a path forward.
policyExit OS · refund matrix · late tier

Self-return after the in-time window steps down to ₹200 (₹300 − ₹100 late). The reduction is stated factually with a clear new deadline — no guilt, no "you've lost ₹100". A recharge here still fully reverses the recall.

Exit OS · Refund Matrix · self-return ₹200 late · Reversibility (Bounded)

chevron_right
7सहायता-पिकअप + पूरा हिसाब (R25–R46)CurrentchatAssisted · ₹200
Real Figma — engineer dispatched. Shows a fully transparent breakdown: सिक्योरिटी ₹300 − घर से पिक ₹50 − देरी ₹50 = ₹200. CTAs: "UPI ID दें" / "इंजीनियर को कॉल करके स्टेटस जानें" / "मुझे वापस नहीं करना".
policyExit OS · transparent deductions · Bounded

Every deduction is itemised — the customer sees exactly why ₹300 became ₹200. This is the Exit OS rule: refund math is never a black box. Bounded zone — a recharge now triggers RECALL_CANCELLED; Wiom absorbs the dispatch cost rather than charging the customer to undo it.

Exit OS · Refund Matrix (pickup −₹50, late −₹50) · RECALL_CANCELLED

chevron_right
8पार्टनर TAT के बाद भी (>R46)CurrentchatLate-late · door open
Real Figma — router not recovered even after the partner TAT, but the customer still acts. Wiom re-confirms pickup, "UPI वेरिफाई: abc@okicici · नेट-बॉक्स वापस होते ही ₹200", with a "इंजीनियर को कॉल करके स्टेटस जानें" track. The door stays open.
policyExit OS · late response stays frictionless

Even past the partner TAT, acting is never punished with a dead-end. Re-confirm, keep the ₹200 commitment, and give a live status path. The relationship is approaching irreversible, but Wiom keeps recovery effortless until the device is physically picked up.

Exit OS · No-Silent-Exit · late-response frictionless

chevron_right
9एज: पिक हो गया, UPI बाकीCurrentchatDEVICE_RECOVERED · refund pendingCM-9 obligation
Real Figma — edge case: partner picked up the router but the customer never submitted a UPI ID, so the refund is blocked. The system proactively reaches out: "आपका नेट-बॉक्स पिक हो गया है · आपका पैसा तैयार है, जल्दी से UPI ID दें".
policyExit OS + CM OS · the ₹300 is a logged obligation, not a lapsed credit

The unpaid refund becomes a CM-9 person-level obligation — it survives the exit, has no long-stop, and dormancy never reduces it (C2/C3). So the system keeps reaching out: "your ₹300 is still with us, we don't want your money blocked." Even if this customer re-books months later, CM OS surfaces the open obligation and it is settled before activation. Obligation-discharge contact continues after exit — never marketing, only the money owed.

Exit OS · SD-first · refund-pending → CM-9 obligation ledger (no long-stop)

chevron_right
C · Exit OS safety-nets (new)
10SD-first रिकॉल (और साफ़)ImprovedRecall · SD-firstchat
Fix: lead even harder with the deposit — informational, not persuasive. "Your ₹300 is blocked with us, we don't want that." Self-return (full ₹300) offered first.
SD-first
10:00wifisignal_cellular_altbattery_full

policyExit OS · SD-first · No-Silent-Exit

Every recall must lead with the security deposit — never a generic "return your device". Informational, not persuasive: no urgency, no guilt. Delivered multi-channel (SMS + WhatsApp), not app-only.

Exit OS · SD-first principle · INV-EXIT-04 (No-Silent-Exit)

policyExit OS · Reversibility

Calm zone: recharge → instant full reversal. Bounded zone (pickup dispatched): recharge → RECALL_CANCELLED, Wiom absorbs the dispatch cost. Late response is frictionless — zero fee, no re-onboarding, no posture penalty.

Exit OS · Reversibility · RECALL_CANCELLED

policyExit OS · custody with proof

DEVICE_RECOVERED requires full pickup proof (same netbox_id + intact condition + scan/photo/OTP + system marking). 27% of pickups today are never marked → silent limbo. Proof + a real status fixes that. Incomplete → CUSTODY_DISPUTED (7-day SLA, defaults to customer).

Exit OS · custody state machine · DEVICE_RECOVERED

policyExit OS · closure bookend · C4 (with RS + CM)

The second bookend: "Your refund will be processed in 3–5 days" — never "are you sure you want to leave?". Multi-channel so it lands even if the app is uninstalled. RS OS reflects EXITED for classification completeness; CM OS retains the person at EXITED_WITH_MEMORY — so if they return, it's a clean "welcome back" with context, never "we noticed you left" surveillance framing. After EXITED_FINAL the customer is off all marketing; only obligation-discharge contact is allowed.

Exit OS · EXITED_FINAL · two bookends · C4 · RS reflects / CM EXITED_WITH_MEMORY

verified

Home status spine, from the live Figma (CA Final Dev → Jan 2026, "5 states of system" spec). The home-screen sticky status banner ("घर का नेट: रमेश · स्थिति: …") is the one surface a customer sees on every visit — it must always state the truth of where they are in the lifecycle. The Figma locks 5 canonical states (Booking pending → Installation pending → Net active → Net not active → Discontinued). The spec's own design note — "if some truth blocks continuation, we can have another status as an unblocker" — is exactly where the OSes plug in: when SQ says the net is broken, or Continuity holds a reservation, or Exit is refunding, the banner becomes a truthful unblocker, never a dead end. Phase A = real Figma banners (Current); Phase B = the OS unblockers/reframes. Scroll sideways →

Lifecycle status spine — one banner per stage
BOOKING → INSTALL
बुकिंग अधूरी / सेटअप बाकी · B2I
ACTIVE ⇄ INACTIVE
चालू है / रुका हुआ है · Continuity
UNBLOCKER
नेट टूटा → ठीक कर रहे हैं · SQ+Recovery
REFUND → DISCONTINUED
रिफंड शुरू / सेवा बंद · Exit
OSes running on this flow
B2I — booking-pending, install-pending statuses (1·2) Continuity — active / inactive (reservation, not "stopped") (3·4·8) SQ + Recovery — broken-net unblocker status (7) Exit — refund-in-progress, discontinued (5·6·9) RS OS — decides which truth the banner reflects CM — the person behind "घर का नेट: रमेश"
A · Lifecycle status spine (live Figma)
1बुकिंग अधूरी हैCurrentB2I · booking-pendingchat-home
Real Figma — booking started but not finished (address not submitted / serviceability pending). Banner CTA drives completion: "बुकिंग पूरी करें".

policyB2I OS · the banner is the status hub

From the first moment, the home banner reflects the real B2I state (S0–S2) and gives the one next action — "complete booking". No empty home, no "coming soon" — the status surface is honest and actionable from day zero.

B2I OS · booking states S0–S2 · status hub

policyB2I OS · install states S3–S9

"सेटअप बाकी है" covers everything from partner-assigned to slot-confirmed to in-progress. The timer makes the wait bounded and trustworthy rather than open-ended — the status hub ("कब लगेगा") is the B2I backbone.

policyContinuity OS · expired → reservation

Net off does not mean the relationship ended — for 15 days it's a reservation, recharge restarts instantly. The Figma wording "रुका हुआ है" reads like a cut-off; Continuity's reframe (screen 8) keeps the truth but removes the dread.

Continuity OS · Reservation T+0–15 (reframed in #8)

B · OS unblockers & reframes (new)
7नेट टूटा है — हम ठीक कर रहे हैंNewSQ · Unusable · Wiom-sideRecovery · openunblockerchat-home
New — the spec's own "unblocker": when SQ says the net is broken on Wiom's side, the banner must not say "रुका हुआ है" (which implies the customer lapsed) and must not ask for recharge. It becomes a Recovery status.

send
policySQ + Recovery → status unblocker

This is the spec's "truth that blocks continuation → unblocker status" made real. SQ emits Unusable / Wiom-side; Recovery opens a case; the banner becomes "हम ठीक कर रहे हैं" with validity frozen. The customer is never blamed or billed for Wiom's failure (C2).

SQ Unusable → Recovery open → banner unblocker · C2
